mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of retail sales and housing starts shows no predictive power, with the interaction term p-value exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized signals robust supply-side expansion that supports corporate output and generates positive short-term equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when core inflation exceeds 3.0 percent, causing direction flips across regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5 percent year-over-year triggers market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of high-inflation months, refuting the tightening channel.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate signals labor market inefficiency and hidden economic weakness, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern shows no statistical edge, with Rank IC falling below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above 2.5 percent annualized sustains underlying corporate earnings expansion, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Three-year rolling Rank IC falls below 0.03, indicating the GDP signal lacks persistent predictive power for equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 105 signals capacity utilization near peak levels, creating supply bottlenecks that constrain growth and trigger negative equity returns as markets price in future slowdowns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, invalidating the capacity constraint mechanism.
